title: 2016 Italian Constitutional Referendum on the Renzi-Boschi Reform

polling_trajectory.source: Estimated: Based on average of Ipsos/Demos polls from early Sept 2016
Estimated: Trend showing slight narrowing of gap
Estimated: Shift toward No camp following Renzi's public debates
Estimated: Momentum shift after official campaign launch
Estimated: Final polling before blackout period

events.description: Renzi announces the date of the referendum, formalizing the campaign period.
Former PM Berlusconi officially declares his opposition to the reform.
Market volatility increases as international investors express concern over political stability.

agents.name: Matteo Renzi
agents.description: Prime Minister and main proponent of the constitutional reform.

agents.name: Partito Democratico (PD)
agents.description: Governing party supporting the Yes vote.

agents.name: Movimento 5 Stelle (M5S)
agents.description: Main opposition party campaigning for No.

agents.name: Lega Nord
agents.description: Right-wing populist party strongly opposing the reform.

agents.name: Forza Italia
agents.description: Center-right party that shifted to oppose the reform.

agents.name: Italian Youth Cluster
agents.description: Demographic group generally leaning toward reform but susceptible to anti-establishment sentiment.

notes: Pro-side (SI) refers to voters supporting the constitutional reform. Against-side (NO) refers to voters opposing the reform. Data quality: Polling data is estimated based on historical trends from major Italian pollsters (Ipsos, Demos) as specific round-by-round micro-data is not publicly archived in a single unified dataset.
